description: Production-grade epic generation from 12 FRs — groups into capability clusters with correct traceability and dependencies

items.title: Expense Capture & Processing
items.description: Enable users to capture expenses via receipt photography with automatic data extraction and categorisation

features.title: Receipt Photo Capture
features.description: Camera integration for capturing receipt images with crop and quality validation
features.acceptance_summary: User can photograph a receipt; image is stored locally and queued for processing

features.title: OCR Data Extraction
features.description: Automatic extraction of amount, date, and vendor from receipt images using OCR
features.acceptance_summary: Amount, date, and vendor extracted with >90% accuracy; user can correct errors

features.title: Auto-Categorisation Engine
features.description: Automatic expense categorisation into predefined categories with user override
features.acceptance_summary: Expenses auto-categorised; user can recategorise; categories are extensible

metadata.reasoning: FR-01, FR-02, FR-03 form a natural capability cluster — all relate to getting expense data into the system. They share a common user journey: photo → extract → categorise.

trajectory.detail: Identified capture/processing cluster from FR-01 to FR-03
These form a single user journey — capture to categorise
Created epic with 3 features, one per FR

items.title: Expense Customisation
items.description: Allow users to extend the expense system with custom categories

features.title: Custom Category CRUD
features.description: Create, edit, delete custom expense categories
features.acceptance_summary: User manages custom categories beyond the predefined set

features.title: Category Merge & Remap
features.description: Merge custom categories or remap expenses when categories change
features.acceptance_summary: Existing expenses can be remapped when categories are reorganised

metadata.reasoning: FR-04 (custom categories) is a standalone extension of the categorisation system. Separated from core capture (EPIC-01) as it's Should-Have and independently deliverable.

trajectory.detail: Alerts require budgets — same epic. Dashboard adds visibility.

items.title: Reporting & Tax Compliance
items.description: Generate monthly/quarterly reports, export as PDF/CSV, and produce HMRC-aligned year-end tax summaries

features.title: Monthly & Quarterly Report Generation
features.description: Automated report generation with category breakdowns and trends
features.acceptance_summary: Reports generated monthly/quarterly with totals and trend comparisons

features.title: Report Export (PDF/CSV)
features.description: Export reports in PDF and CSV formats for accountants
features.acceptance_summary: User exports report as PDF or CSV suitable for accountant

features.title: Year-End HMRC Tax Summary
features.description: Generate tax summary matching HMRC self-assessment categories
features.acceptance_summary: Year-end report maps expenses to HMRC categories

metadata.reasoning: FR-05, FR-06, FR-07 form a natural reporting cluster — all relate to generating and exporting expense summaries. Depends on EPIC-01 (needs categorised expenses to report on). Should-Have per stakeholder guidance.

trajectory.detail: All three are report-related, same priority tier
Created epic with 3 features, one per FR

items.title: Budget Management & Alerts
items.description: Enable users to set spending budgets per category and receive proactive alerts when approaching limits

features.title: Budget Configuration
features.description: Set and manage monthly spending limits per category
features.acceptance_summary: User sets/edits budget per category; persists across sessions

features.title: Budget Threshold Alerts
features.description: Push notifications at 80% and 100% of budget
features.acceptance_summary: User receives push at 80% and 100% thresholds

features.title: Weekly Spending Summary
features.description: Weekly digest notification every Sunday evening
features.acceptance_summary: Weekly summary push sent Sunday evening

metadata.reasoning: FR-08, FR-09, FR-10 are tightly coupled — alerts (FR-09) depend on budgets (FR-08); weekly summary (FR-10) also relates to spending visibility. Depends on EPIC-01 (needs categorised expenses for budget tracking).

trajectory.detail: Alerts depend on budgets; weekly summary is spending visibility
Created 3 features — config, threshold alerts, weekly digest

items.title: Authentication & Security
items.description: User registration, login, and biometric authentication for secure access

features.title: Email/Password Registration & Login
features.description: User account creation and authentication via email/password
features.acceptance_summary: User registers with email/password and can log in

features.title: Biometric Login
features.description: Optional Face ID / fingerprint for returning users
features.acceptance_summary: Returning user logs in via biometric without typing password

metadata.reasoning: FR-11 and FR-12 are both authentication — tightly coupled. No dependencies on other epics (auth is foundational). GDPR compliance (NFR-04) applies to user data handling.

trajectory.detail: Foundational — no dependencies, all other epics need authenticated users

output.execution_summary:
• Produced 5 epics with 13 features total
• Requirements coverage: 12/12 FRs assigned (100%)
• Grouping strategy: capability clusters (capture → customisation → reporting → budget → auth)
• Key decisions: Auth (EPIC-05) has no dependencies — foundational. Reporting/budget both depend on EPIC-01 (need categorised data). Custom categories split from core capture (different priority).
• Reflection: initially grouped FR-04 with EPIC-01 — split into separate epic as it's Should-Have and independently deliverable. Verified all 12 FRs covered.
• Dependencies: EPIC-05 (auth) is independent and should be delivered first. EPIC-01 (capture) next. EPIC-02, 03, 04 can run in parallel after EPIC-01.
